title: 中考六选五 4: When Advice Becomes Pressure
passage:
Advice is common in teenage life. Parents, teachers and friends often tell students what they should do. Some advice is useful and comes from care. 1. ______ When this happens, advice may no longer feel like help.

Good advice usually respects the listener’s situation. It does not simply say, “You must do this.” Instead, it helps the person see choices more clearly. 2. ______ The final decision should still belong to the person who has to live with the result.

Pressure is different. Pressure often uses fear, comparison or guilt. For example, someone may say, “Everyone else is doing this, so you should too.” 3. ______ It may push students to follow a path that is not suitable for them.

Students should learn to listen without losing judgment. They can ask themselves: Is this advice based on facts? Does the person understand my situation? 4. ______ These questions help students separate useful suggestions from emotional pressure.

At the same time, rejecting advice too quickly is also unwise. Some advice feels uncomfortable because it points out a real weakness. 5. ______ A mature student does not accept everything, but also does not ignore everything.

questions.label: Questions 1-5 - Sentence Insertion
questions.instruction: Choose the best sentence from A-F for each blank in the passage. There is one extra sentence you do not need.

choices.text: In other words, advice should support thinking, not replace it.
But advice can become pressure when people leave no room for choice.
This kind of language can make students feel trapped rather than guided.
So the best choice is to avoid asking others for advice.
Will following it help me in the long run?
Therefore, students should judge the message instead of only reacting to the feeling.
